meaning: n.根,根部,根本,根源v.(使)生根,(使)扎根,使立定不动,坚定不移,确立
examples: We got to the root of the problem.
Most plants will root in about six to eight weeks.
There would be a major drive to root out corruption.
Either way, the saplings took root and flourished, and eventually grew into a cathedral grove.
Bad regulations are always at the root of black markets and the criminality they spawn.
